task_completion.description: Verify that the three required ground stations (Wallops, Bermuda_Downrange, Antigua_Downrange) have been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the names 'Wallops', 'Bermuda_Downrange', and 'Antigua_Downrange' in the 'Edit Ground Stations' dialog, the module configuration window, or marked as locations on the map view.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a new module named COLA_Monitoring.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a tab or window title in the GPredict interface that is explicitly named 'COLA_Monitoring'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the COLA_Monitoring module is configured to use Wallops as its ground station.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the COLA_Monitoring module's header, status area, or configuration dialog. The active ground station (often labeled QTH) should be set to 'Wallops'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the required crewed space stations are tracked in the COLA_Monitoring module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the List view or map within the COLA_Monitoring module. You should see 'ISS (ZARYA)', 'ISS (POISK)', 'ISS (NAUKA)', and 'CSS (TIANHE)' listed or tracked.

task_completion.description: Verify the layout of the COLA_Monitoring module contains exactly the three specified views.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the COLA_Monitoring module interface. It must display exactly three distinct panels simultaneously: a Map view (world map), a Polar view (circular radar-like display), and a List view (table of satellites).

task_completion.description: Verify that GPredict is configured to display UTC time instead of local time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the clock display in the GPredict interface (usually in the module header or main status bar). It should explicitly show 'UTC' next to the time.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GPredict graphical interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for open GPredict dialog boxes (e.g., 'Edit Ground Stations', 'New Module', 'Preferences') in the screenshots, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual GPredict application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the standard GPredict application window with its characteristic map, polar, and list views, rather than a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the GUI by directly editing GPredict's configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no terminal windows showing SQLite commands, python scripts, or text editors modifying files in the `.config/Gpredict/` directory.
